A TORNADO TRACKED ACROSS FAR SOUTHERN MAHNOMEN FOR ROUGHLY 400 YARDS OVER A 30 TO 40 SECOND PERIOD. THIS TORNADO RIPPED ROOFING OFF OF A MAINTENANCE SHED... LIFTED AND TIPPED A PARKED SEMI TRAILER...SLID PARKED VEHICLES...AND DESTROYED A SMALL WOODEN SHED. FLYING DEBRIS IMPACTED AND DAMAGED ADDITIONAL VEHICLES AND STRUCTURES NEAR THE CASINO. A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR BARNEY AND TRACKED SOUTH SOUTHEAST FROM A MILE OR TWO BY 826 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BROKE DOWN LARGE TREES AND NUMEROUS LARGE TREE BRANCHES. IT ALSO FLIPPED AND TOSSED A LARGE WOODEN PLAY CENTER FOR SEVERAL YARDS AND JUMBLED SEPTIC TANK PVC PIPES WHICH EXTENDED WESTWARD FROM THE HOUSE...AT GROUND LEVEL. PEAK WINDS EXTIMATED TO 90 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 75 YARDS.

TORNADO WAS INITIALLY REPORTED AS POSSIBLY TWO FUNNEL CLOUDS UNDER THE SAME WALL CLOUD BY A TRAINED SPOTTER. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AND TRACKED FOR LESS THAN A MILE AND LIFTED BY 845 PM CDT. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND A STEEL GRAIN BIN WAS BLOWN OFF ITS FOUNDATION AT ONE FARMSTEAD. A NEIGHBORING FARMSTEAD ALSO HAD NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES AND TREE LIMBS BROKEN DOWN. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 90 MPH. MAXIMUM WIDTH TO 100 YARDS.

THIS TORNADO BEGAN AROUND 1036PM AND ENDED AROUND 1040PM IN THE TOWN OF TOLNA. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS ARE AROUND 110 MPH...WHICH IS AN EF-1 RATING. DAMAGE INCLUDED SEVERAL UPROOTED TREES...A BENT TOWER...AND A QUONSET STORAGE BUILDING BLOWN OFF ITS FOUNDATION. MUCH OF THE DAMAGE OCCURRED IN THE VICINITY OF LEE AVENUE ON THESOUTH SIDE OF TOWN...INCLUDING THE CITY PARK AREA. THE ESTIMATED PATH WAS AROUND 0.5 MILES LONG AND 350 YARDS WIDE. A POWER OUTAGE OCCURRED AS SOON AS THE TORNADO HIT.
HIGHWAY SUPERINTENDANT REPORTED TREES FALLING ONTO ROOF OF CITY SHOP. ROW OF TREES DOWN WITHIN CITY PARK. 60 BY 40 FOOT BUILDING SOUTH END OF TOWN TOTALLY DEMOLISHED.

EM RELAYED THE FOLLOWING...FARMERS UNION - EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO A LEAN TO. MINOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL GARAGES. BUILDING ON A HOMEOWNERS PROPERTY SUFFERED TOTAL LOSS. NUMEROUS TREES DAMAGED THROUGHOUT THE CITY.
